title: Hippeastrum incachacanum
taxonomy: Hippeastrum incachacanum is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. It is a bulbous perennial native to the Andes in South America. This species is classified as part of the Amaryllidaceae family, specifically within the subfamily Amaryllidoideae and tribe Hippeastreae. Within the tribe Hippeastreae, Hippeastrum incachacanum is further classified within the genus Hippeastrum. The exact classification of Hippeastrum incachacanum may vary depending on the source.

intro: Hippeastrum incachacanum is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, commonly known as the Inca lily or Incachacanum lily. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and studies have shown that it possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Hippeastrum incachacanum include alkaloids, such as galanthamine and lycorine, which have been found to have potential neuroprotective and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations made from the plant, including infusions and extracts, have been traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues.
